10	// cmdSetProcessGroup configures cmd so that it and all its children share a
11	// new process group (Setpgid) and are automatically killed when the parent
12	// thread dies (Pdeathsig). This ensures that if the parent worker dies
13	// abnormally (SIGKILL, crash, OOM), the harness child is reaped by the kernel
14	// rather than orphaning to PID 1.
15	//
16	// Pdeathsig is sent when the creating thread dies, not the process, so the
17	// caller must use runtime.LockOSThread around cmd.Start() to prevent Go
18	// runtime from migrating the goroutine to a different OS thread. See
19	// golang.org/issue/27505 for details.
20	//
21	// Note: Pdeathsig is Linux-only. macOS and Windows do not support it; the
22	// graceful shutdown path (cmdKillProcessGroup on context cancellation) serves
23	// as the fallback for those platforms, and the orphan reaper is the final
24	// backstop for already-dead workers.
25	func cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd *exec.Cmd) {
26		cmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{
27			Setpgid:   true,
28			Pdeathsig: syscall.SIGKILL,
29		}
30	}
31	
32	// cmdKillProcessGroup sends SIGKILL to the entire process group of cmd.
33	// Because cmd was started with Setpgid=true, its PID equals its PGID, so
34	// passing -pid kills all descendants that inherited the group.
35	func cmdKillProcessGroup(cmd *exec.Cmd) {
36		if cmd.Process == nil {
37			return
38		}
39		_ = syscall.Kill(-cmd.Process.Pid, syscall.SIGKILL)
40	}

When a `ddx work` worker dies abnormally while an execute-bead harness is running, the direct harness child can reparent to PID 1 and continue burning a harness session. This was observed on 2026-05-27 with a `claude --print` execute-bead child orphaned under PPID 1 for about 9h53m.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/executor.go:156 spawns the harness with exec.Command(binary, args...) and then calls cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd).
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:13-15 sets only SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}; it does not configure a parent-death signal.
- cli/cmd/root.go:49-54 handles os.Interrupt and SIGTERM only. SIGKILL, crashes, OOM, and parent process death cannot run the normal defer/stopProcess cleanup.
- cli/internal/agent/executor.go:190-194 and cli/internal/agent/executor.go:231-255 only kill the process group when graceful executor paths run.

PROPOSED FIX

The docker-clone attempt backend has a separate leak vector from the normal executor path. Even when graceful cleanup runs, the docker invocation may not be in the process group/container cleanup path that execute-bead harness children rely on, so nested harness work inside the container can survive worker exit or leave containers behind.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:293 starts `exec.CommandContext(ctx, "docker", ...)` directly for docker-clone backend execution.
- That docker command does not call cmdSetProcessGroup, unlike local/worktree clone paths that flow through cli/internal/agent/executor.go:156.
- The parent bead notes local-clone/worktree backends at cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:120-122 and cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:182-184 share the executor path and get Setpgid, while docker-clone does not.
- Process cleanup therefore depends on CommandContext and Docker behavior alone, which may not terminate nested harness processes or remove containers in abnormal and graceful worker-exit cases.

PROPOSED FIX
- Apply cmdSetProcessGroup or an equivalent process-group cleanup wrapper to the docker invocation at cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:293.
- Ensure docker containers are started with cleanup semantics such as `--rm` where appropriate and that graceful executor cleanup stops/removes the container when the worker exits.
- If Docker already guarantees nested harness teardown under the chosen invocation, add a documented justification and regression test proving the guarantee.
- Keep the local-clone/worktree executor cleanup behavior unchanged.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add Linux Pdeathsig handling in this child; that is covered by the executor parent-death child bead.
- Do not implement general `ddx work` startup orphan reaping in this child; that is covered by the startup reaper child bead and cross-ref ddx-8f2e0ebf.

- The harness-runner-internal leak in fizeau is covered by fizeau-50b6f055; this child remains at the ddx orchestrator -> harness-child boundary.
